questions.text: What is the output of 1 AND 0?
questions.explanation: AND outputs 1 only when BOTH inputs are 1. Since one input is 0, the output is 0.

questions.text: What is the output of 0 OR 1?
questions.explanation: OR outputs 1 when AT LEAST ONE input is 1. Since one input is 1, the output is 1.

questions.text: What is the output of NOT 1?
questions.explanation: NOT inverts its input. NOT 1 = 0, NOT 0 = 1.

questions.text: What is the output of 1 XOR 1?
questions.explanation: XOR outputs 1 only when inputs are DIFFERENT. When both inputs are 1 (the same), the output is 0.
Step 1: 1 AND 1 = 1. Step 2: NOT 0 = 1. Step 3: 1 OR 1 = 1. Result: 1.

questions.text: How many rows are in a truth table with 2 inputs?
questions.explanation: A truth table with n inputs has 2ⁿ rows. With 2 inputs: 2² = 4 rows (covering all combinations: 00, 01, 10, 11).
NAND is the inverse of AND. AND outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1, so NAND outputs 0 only when all inputs are 1.
NOR is the inverse of OR. OR outputs 0 only when all inputs are 0, so NOR outputs 1 only when all inputs are 0.
George Boole (1815–1864) developed the system of symbolic logic now called Boolean algebra. Claude Shannon later applied it to electrical circuits in 1937.

questions.text: Evaluate: NOT (A AND B) when A=1, B=1
questions.explanation: Step 1: A AND B = 1 AND 1 = 1. Step 2: NOT 1 = 0. Result: 0. (This is the NAND operation.)

questions.text: Which gate is described as 'universal' because all other gates can be built from it?
questions.explanation: NAND is a universal gate — every other logic gate (AND, OR, NOT, XOR) can be constructed using only NAND gates. NOR is also universal.
